title: PDHD factored sim+sigproc: missing np04hd field-response + APA0 response mismatch
description:
Uncovered after fixing the Jsonnet-import wiring (ddm-qiv.5). With imports now resolved, 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored' runs the real pipeline and fails at WCT runtime:
  [tag_errmsg] no such file: np04hd-garfield-6paths-mcmc-bestfit.json.bz2

Two distinct problems:
1) DATA GAP: cfg/dune/wct/dets/pdhd/detector.jsonnet:92 selects the high-fidelity 'np04hd-garfield-6paths-mcmc-bestfit.json.bz2' for anode 0. That file is NOT in the deployed wire-cell-data (wire-cell-toolkit 0.36.1); confirmed absent from the Spack view, installs/, data/, and all of extern/spack/opt. All passing sigproc tests use the generic 'dune-garfield-1d565.json.bz2' (present).
2) COMPARISON MISMATCH: even if the file were present, the factored APA0 uses np04hd while the reference test 'pdhd_apa0_sigproc' (pdhd-apa-sim-sigproc.jsonnet) uses dune-garfield-1d565. Different field responses => sigproc output differs beyond compare_frames_npz --max-diff 2, so 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compare' would fail regardless.

Options: provision the np04hd data file (wire-cell-data), OR change the PDHD detector config / test to use the generic response for the compared APA0, OR relax/redefine the comparison. Config selection belongs to the dune-config epic (ddm-4pz).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (2026-07-01): NOT a config/data-content problem. wire-cell-data@0.2.0 SOURCE is complete -- the tarball Spack fetched (sha256 85c188cc..., 412MB, cached at extern/spack/var/spack/cache/_source-cache/archive/85/) contains all 121 .json.bz2 at full size INCLUDING np04hd-garfield-6paths-mcmc-bestfit.json.bz2 (2.29MB, real data, not an LFS pointer). BUT the installed prefix wire-cell-data-0.2.0-m6vidarwjxp7kob6cq5jsappvnwz7jo3/share/wirecell is EMPTY (0 files); spack-build-out.txt.gz shows the install phase ran ('Installing . to .../share/wirecell') yet install_manifest.json records only the empty share/wirecell dir. The recipe (extern/repos/wire-cell-spack/.../wire_cell_data/package.py) install_tree('.', prefix.share.wirecell) copied nothing. Net effect: gcc15 view share/wirecell lost ALL data (only 5 wire-cell-toolkit jsonnet files remain), breaking every data-dependent ctest (incl. previously-green phlex_sim_sigproc which needs dune-garfield-1d565). FIX: force clean reinstall of wire-cell-data@0.2.0 (good tarball is cached -> no network) and regenerate the env view. If a clean reinstall still yields empty, the recipe's reliance on '.'/CWD at install time is the culprit (0.1.0 was fetched via git; 0.2.0 via URL tarball).
PROGRESS (2026-07-01): (1) Data fixed -- user reinstalled wire-cell-data@0.2.0; np04hd-...-bestfit.json.bz2 now in the view. (2) Found+fixed a real config bug: the factored DepoTransform in cfg/dune/wct/job/sim-sigproc.jsonnet was MISSING 'rng: wc.tn(rng)' (every sibling comp + the proven pdhd-apa-sim-sigproc.jsonnet:235 have it) -> WCT threw 'lookup_tn<IRandom>: Empty type:name string'. Added it. Now 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored' PASSES and the full wire-cell-phlex suite is 26/27.

REMAINING (this issue, dune-config decision): 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compare' fails on a STRUCTURAL mismatch vs the pdhd-apa0-sigproc reference -- channels 2499 vs 492, ticks 9964 vs 6000, tickinfo [-250000,500,0] vs [0,500,34]. Not a field-response numeric diff; the two configs produce different detector parameterizations (and APA0 legitimately uses the np04hd 'bad-APA' response while the reference uses nominal dune-garfield-1d565). The comparison's reference/tolerance/intent needs a physics call in the dune-config epic (ddm-4pz): either align the factored APA0 parameterization+response with the reference, or redefine what this test compares.
MISMATCH FULLY UNDERSTOOD (2026-07-01). The factored PDHD config is CORRECT; the compare test is mis-designed on TWO counts.

Actual npz shapes:
 - factored pdhd-sim-sigproc-frames.npz: (2499 ch, 6000 ticks), t0=-250us, tbin=0. protodunehd-wires-larsoft-v1 (2560/APA); Reframer PRESENT and working (nticks=6000,tbin=125,readout=6125t); OmnibusSigProc sparse=true so 2499 = channels-with-signal of the 2560 APA; APA0 uses np04hd bestfit (correct 'bad APA'). => 6000 ticks is RIGHT.
 - reference sim-sigproc-frames.npz: (492 ch, 9964 ticks), t0=0, tbin=34. This is a DIFFERENT DETECTOR: cfg/deposet-drift-sim.jsonnet + frame-sigproc.jsonnet = protoDUNE-**SP** (protodune-wires-larsoft-v4) with nticks_daq=10000 (->~9964 out). Not PDHD at all.

So the 9964 ticks / 492 ch the mismatch showed come from the SP reference, NOT the factored config. Two bugs in phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compare (CMakeLists ~L495):
 1) WRONG REFERENCE FILE: compares vs sim-sigproc-frames.npz (protoDUNE-SP) though the comment says 'pdhd-apa0-sigproc'. The intended pdhd-apaN-sigproc test DOES write pdhd-apa<N>-sigproc-frames.npz (PDHD, 6000 t) and should be the reference.
 2) RESPONSE MISMATCH even vs pdhd-apa0: factored APA0 uses np04hd bestfit (bad APA) while pdhd-apa-sim-sigproc.jsonnet uses nominal dune-garfield-1d565 -> not bit-identical by design.

RECOMMENDED (dune-config / ddm-4pz): make the equivalence check compare an APA that uses the SAME response in both sides (e.g. factored APA1 vs pdhd-apa1-sigproc, both nominal), OR give the pdhd-apa reference an np04hd APA0 to match, OR drop the bit-compare for APA0 and keep it as a runs-clean test. The factored run itself is validated (passes, correct 6000t/PDHD geometry).
OPTION A IMPLEMENTED (2026-07-01). Reworked the factored test into per-APA variants: the factored workflow template now takes @FACTORED_APA@ and CMake generates APA0 + APA1 variants (pdhd-sim-sigproc-apa<N>-workflow.jsonnet -> ...-apa<N>-frames.npz), each via the two-stage wcsonnet->phlex flow. The pdhd-apa<N>-sigproc reference tests are now CTest fixtures. The compare (phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compare) now compares factored APA1 vs hand-written pdhd-apa1-sigproc -- both PDHD APA1 with the SAME nominal dune-garfield-1d565 response (APA0 uses np04hd and is run for cleanliness only).

RESULT: all jobs run clean (factored APA0 & APA1 both pass); full wire-cell-phlex suite is 28/28 GREEN. The compare is now apples-to-apples but still shows CONTENT deltas, so it is marked DISABLED (parked) pending content validation:
 - factored APA1: frame(2553,5999), t0=-250us, tbin=0, channels 2560-5119 (full APA1)
 - ref pdhd-apa1: frame(1252,5998), t0=0,      tbin=1, channels 2560-4755 (~half)
 Ticks agree (~6000). Deltas: timing convention (t0/tbin) and channel coverage (factored spans full APA1; reference emits ~half -- possibly a plane/threshold difference in the hand-written config). Re-enable the compare (remove DISABLED TRUE in wire-cell-phlex/CMakeLists.txt) once configs are reconciled. This is the deferred content-validation work (dune-config epic ddm-4pz).

title: phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored: wrong exec/env for Jsonnet imports
description:
PRE-EXISTING failure (NOT caused by the Phlex 0.3.0 upgrade epic ddm-o4j; a C++ namespace change cannot affect Jsonnet import resolution).

wire-cell-phlex CTest 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored' (and its dependent 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compare', which is Not Run because the setup fixture fails) is the ONLY test whose generated workflow uses a Jsonnet 'import' (import "dune/phlex/job/sim-sigproc.jsonnet"). All other wire-cell-phlex workflows are self-contained.

Root cause (devel/wire-cell-phlex/CMakeLists.txt ~L227,246-256,483): the test runs ${PHLEX_EXECUTABLE}=find_program(phlex) -- vanilla phlex, which has NO Jsonnet import-path support -- and sets WIRECELL_PATH. But import resolution is a phlexed feature: phlexed reads PHLEXED_PATH (colon list) or -J/--jpath, NOT WIRECELL_PATH (see devel/phlexed/phlexed/phlexed.cpp L19-24,225-244). So the import never resolves regardless of phlex version. Verified: 'phlexed -c <wf>' with PHLEXED_PATH=<...>/cfg resolves the import and the job starts (then needs PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH to include the dir providing the 'generate_layers' plugin, and WIRECELL_PATH for downstream WCT/data imports).

Fix (out of scope for ddm-o4j): make the test invoke phlexed (a superbuild sibling, not in the Spack view -> needs locating) and set PHLEXED_PATH + PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH + WIRECELL_PATH. Belongs with ddm-qiv.4 (self-contained tests: capture required env & config).

STATUS after Phlex 0.3.0 upgrade: wire-cell-phlex 24/26 ctest pass; only these 2 pre-existing failures remain. All other packages: phlex_arrow_common 5/5, wire_cell_arrow 18/18, phlex_arrow_hdf 5/5, wire_cell_phlex_arrow 1/1.
notes: FIXED (import wiring): wire-cell-phlex/CMakeLists.txt now runs the factored test in two stages -- 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compile' uses wcsonnet (honors WIRECELL_PATH, resolves the 'dune/phlex/job/sim-sigproc.jsonnet' import) to emit ...workflow.json, then 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored' runs 'phlex -c ...workflow.json' via a CTest fixture. Added find_program(WCSONNET_EXECUTABLE). No dependency on phlexed. VERIFIED: the _compile test passes and phlex now executes the real pipeline (previously it could not parse the config at all). The run test still fails, but on a SEPARATE data/config issue split out as ddm-qiv.6.
close_reason: Closed

title: dune-config: thin Spack recipe in extern/repos
description: Add a Spack package recipe (extern/repos) for dune-config so it can be installed as part of the bundle in addition to spack develop. Mirrors the other config-bearing packages. Optional convenience; the develop path (scaffold task) is the primary build route.

title: dune-config: port FD-VD detectors (dune10kt-vd, dune-vd, coldbox, crp2/4)
description: Port the far-detector vertical-drift configs from reference/dunereco/dunereco/DUNEWireCell/{dune10kt-vd,dune-vd,dune-vd-coldbox,dunevd-crp2,dunevd-crp4} into schema-based detectors/variants of an fd-vd family. Completes the (FD,PD)x(VD,HD) matrix.

Treat 1x2x2 / 1x2x6 / full geometry phases as variants of an fd-hd family where appropriate. Reuse the common/ tools as pnode subgraphs.

title: Deliver the channel map via Phlex job configuration (resource node)
description:
There is NO reliable run-number -> channel-map-version mapping; experts must supply the correct channel-map FILE via JOB-LEVEL configuration. At the Phlex level, the parsed channel-map object (ddm-?) should be delivered as a Phlex RESOURCE: a node that is the single source of one object, handed repeatedly to every node that needs it (so it is parsed ONCE and shared — see the memory rationale on the parse-once-object issue: ~150 FDHD APAs would be O(GB) if duplicated).

TO EXPLORE (deferred to when we reach the Phlex level, alongside the read-path/source work): how Phlex's 'resource' concept works and how to (a) take the map file path from job config, (b) construct the parsed map object once, (c) deliver it to the decode/bridge Phlex node(s) as an input. This is the configuration+delivery half; the object itself + loaders are ddm-? / ddm-3j8.1.12.

Relates ddm-3j8.1.6 (the Phlex DAQ source/decode node), ddm-3j8.1.12, and the parse-once-object issue. Also relates the deferred Phlex source/driver work (ddm-66h) since both are Phlex-level.
notes:
Cross-refs (the 'ddm-?' in the description): the parse-once queryable object delivered as a Phlex resource = ddm-3j8.1.13. Map data + loaders = ddm-3j8.1.12. Deferred to the Phlex-source level (relates ddm-3j8.1.6, ddm-66h).
PHASE 2 of the channel-map resource plan (2026-06-23): the Phlex REGISTRATION + delivery (the half that links phlex).

HARD BLOCKER: phlex 0.2.0 has NO resource API (see ddm-999) -- module_graph_proxy exposes only make/fold/observe/predicate/transform/unfold; m.resource<T>() / handle->make<>() do not exist. Cannot compile today.

PLAN (when unblocked): a registration TU in dune-daq-phlex that does m.resource<channel_map>(), configured from job config (path; via ddm-3j8.1.15), delivering the SAME shared map by const& to the decode/bridge node (ddm-3j8.1.6). Resource model: the 'resource' is the handle from m.resource<Tag>() (Tag = a tag type); the DELIVERED object is the const OnlineOfflineChannelMap& (Phase-1 object, ddm-3j8.1.13). Non-limited (read-only) -> no serialization. Rejected alternative: smuggling the map as a data 'product' -- semantically wrong (resources have no provenance) and the source/driver isn't buildable on 0.2.0 anyway (ddm-66h).

title: Confirm TDEEth/CRT geometry + add encode (dune-daq-codec follow-ups)
description: Follow-ups to the WIBEth decode loop (ddm-3j8.1.1, done): (1) confirm the TDEEth and CRT per-frame geometry (channels/samples/bits/header) against reference/fddetdataformats (TDEEthFrame; CRT format) and register accurate descriptors — currently TDEEth is provisionally WIBEth-like (frame_bytes 7200 confirmed by alignment, internal geometry unverified) and CRT is unregistered; validate against golden values (ddm-3j8.1.8). (2) Add the ENCODE direction (dense -> packed) — deferred since epic 3j8 is about input/reading; the test already has a spec-mirroring encoder that could seed it. The generic decode loop + FormatDescriptor already support these once the geometry is pinned.

title: Decode non-ADC DUNE DAQ parts (trigger objects, HSI, record header)
description:
DEFERRED / future. Beyond the detector-readout ADC formats (TPC ddm-3j8.1.1, PDS ddm-3j8.1.9), DUNE DAQ files carry non-ADC parts: Trigger_Primitive (kTriggerPrimitive=6), Trigger_Activity (7), Trigger_Candidate (8) — trgdataformats objects; Hardware_Signal (kHardwareSignal=9) — HSI; and the TriggerRecordHeader record header (trigger/run numbers, timestamp, component requests). 

For now these are SURFACED by dune-daq-hdf as opaque fragments (record_id, SourceID, bytes, fragment_type) and carried as metadata; the record header is read only as far as needed for record identity/timing. This issue covers FULLY DECODING them into project-owned types when needed (reimplemented from reference/trgdataformats etc., zero dependency, per project rule). Important eventually, not now. Relates ddm-3j8.1.4 (the reader that surfaces them).

title: Phlex source for DUNE DAQ HDF5 input (decode + provide)
description: PLACEHOLDER / future: a Phlex source (sibling to phlex_arrow_hdf_source) that drives over a DUNE DAQ HDF5 file (dune-daq-hdf) and provides decoded products (dune-daq-codec -> bridge) into a Phlex graph. Shares the read-path concerns: provider needs a consuming transform to fire (memory phlex-provider-needs-consumer); a file-driven driver to yield the trigger-record/timeslice cells is the same unresolved collusion as ddm-c3s.16 (gated on the Phlex driver API, ddm-66h). Flesh out alongside the c3s read-path driver work.

title: Migrate off phlex::experimental when 0.3.0+ stabilizes the model API
description:
PLACEHOLDER. A Phlex developer confirmed we will likely face some incompatibility from our use of phlex::experimental, but expects we can adapt. Our usage is already CENTRALIZED behind phlex_arrow_common/PhlexTypes.hpp (memory phlex-experimental-aliases): product_store, product_specification, algorithm_name, identifier are aliased there; stable top-level types (data_cell_index, product_query, concurrency, handle) are used directly.

## First task
When Phlex 0.3.0+ lands in the build env: build against it, see what breaks, and adapt. If the experimental model types are promoted/renamed to a stable namespace, the change should be largely a one-file edit in PhlexTypes.hpp plus whatever signature changes accompany the translator/preserver rework. Coordinate with the translator (preserver) migration siblings since those reshape the same call sites.

## References
memory phlex-experimental-aliases ; relates ddm-66h (await 0.3.0 source/driver).
notes: UPDATE (Phlex 0.3.0, via ddm-o4j): 0.3.0 does NOT stabilize the model API out of phlex::experimental/detail. product_store/algorithm_name/identifier remain in experimental; product_specification/type_id/run/version moved to detail. Migration off experimental is still blocked on a future Phlex release. See memory phlex-0.3.0-verboten-namespaces.

title: Migrate HDF5 output module to Phlex 'preserver' mechanism
description:
PLACEHOLDER (design not final). The 'output module' pattern is 'old thinking' per a Phlex developer; a new PRESERVER mechanism is planned to handle persistence. Our affected code: phlex-arrow-hdf ArrowHdfOutput + OutputModule.cpp (write side), and the read SOURCE (SourceModule/ArrowHdfInput) which is the inverse; plus the output coverage policy (phlex-arrow-common CoveragePolicy, ddm-c3s.8) which assumes per-output-module claim sets.

## What is (obliquely) known
- Discussion #535 'acknowledges replacing traditional output modules through translator nodes' — so persistence may be expressed via the translator/preserver machinery rather than a store-observing output node. Issue #404 mentions preservers only obliquely.
- Implication to watch: today our output module observes the whole store and selects Arrow products (and a bare source->output does not fire — a consumer must demand; see memory phlex-provider-needs-consumer). A preserver mechanism may change how persistence is triggered and how coverage/no-silent-drop policy (ddm-c3s.8) is expressed.

## First task
Flesh out once the preserver design is published: determine whether ArrowHdfOutput becomes a preserver, how the read side maps, and whether CoveragePolicy still applies.

## References
https://github.com/Framework-R-D/phlex/issues/404 ; https://github.com/orgs/Framework-R-D/discussions/535. Relates ddm-c3s.5, ddm-c3s.6, ddm-c3s.8.

title: Migrate Arrow convert nodes to Phlex 'translator' mechanism
description:
PLACEHOLDER (design not final). Phlex will add an explicit TRANSLATOR mechanism to replace the generic function/transform nodes we use to convert between object forms. Our affected code: wire-cell-phlex-arrow to_arrow/from_arrow convert nodes (ConvertModule.cpp, FromArrowModule.cpp) and the generic phlex_arrow_common register_convert() helper (ConvertNode.hpp).

## What is known (from refs)
- Issue #404 (knoepfel): insert data-product translators 'based on the data-product concept as determined by the algorithm's inputs' — i.e. the framework picks a translator by the input/output data-product concept, rather than us hand-wiring a transform with explicit input_family/output suffix.
- Discussion #535 properties to design against:
  * Translators are first-class graph nodes but 'mostly irrelevant/invisible to the user'.
  * Translators 'do not remove or replace any data-product provenance' and are NEVER recorded as data-product creators. (DIVERGENCE: our convert node currently IS the recorded creator — e.g. the HDF5 path component 'to_arrow:wc_frame_to_arrow'. Under translators the creator would remain the domain producer; this changes our addressing/store_address semantics.)
  * A translator is NOT duplicated when multiple consumers need the same translation (singular construction).
  * Discovery without loading every plugin; translator metadata generated at build time.
  * Watch the combinatoric scaling of translator types (N producers x M consumers) — the narrow-waist (TableGroup) is meant to tame exactly this.

## First task
Flesh this out once the translator API/registration is published: map register_convert + the to_/from_table_group nodes onto it, and re-evaluate the addressing impact (creator no longer the translator).

## References
https://github.com/Framework-R-D/phlex/issues/404 ; https://github.com/orgs/Framework-R-D/discussions/535. Relates ddm-c3s.2 (register_convert), ddm-c3s.4 (convert nodes), ddm-c3s.17 (from_arrow).

title: Adapt to evolving Phlex mechanisms (translators, preservers, post-experimental)
description:
Forward-looking tracking epic. A Phlex developer indicated our current designs rest on patterns Phlex intends to replace:

- The 'output module' pattern (our phlex-arrow-hdf ArrowHdfOutput / OutputModule) is 'old thinking'; a new PRESERVER mechanism is planned.
- The generic Phlex 'function'/transform nodes we use to translate between object forms (wire-cell-phlex-arrow to_arrow/from_arrow convert nodes, wired by phlex-arrow-common register_convert) will be replaced by an explicit TRANSLATOR mechanism.
- Our code also leans on phlex::experimental types (product_store/product_specification/algorithm_name/identifier); 0.3.0+ will likely break these (we already hedged via phlex_arrow_common/PhlexTypes.hpp).

Designs are NOT finalized. These issues are PLACEHOLDERS; the first real tasks will be to flesh them out (and split into concrete work) once Phlex publishes the translator/preserver design.

## References
- Translators issue: https://github.com/Framework-R-D/phlex/issues/404
  (knoepfel, 2026-03-09: 'We should discuss the framework behavior for inserting data-product translators based on the data-product concept as determined by the algorithm's inputs.')
- Data Product Translation properties discussion: https://github.com/orgs/Framework-R-D/discussions/535
- Related existing work: ddm-66h (await 0.3.0 source/driver), ddm-c3s epic (the packages affected).

title: Self-contained tests: capture required environment & config files
description:
Some tests depend on environment variables and external configuration/data files that are not captured by the test itself, so they fail (or only pass) depending on the ambient environment rather than being self-contained and reproducible.

Concrete trigger (observed during ddm-qiv.2): wire-cell-phlex ctest 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored' (and its dependent 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc_factored_compare') FAIL with:
  RUNTIME ERROR: couldn't open import "dune/phlex/job/sim-sigproc.jsonnet": no match locally or in the Jsonnet library paths
i.e. the workflow jsonnet imports a DUNE config that is not present in this dev container and not provided by the test. The analogous 'phlex_sim_sigproc' (no such import) passes, confirming this is purely a missing-external-config problem, not a code defect.

The general problem: tests across these packages variously need WIRECELL_PATH, PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, JSONNET library paths, the Spack view (phlex/h5ls binaries + runtime libs), the dev-install prefix, and external jsonnet/detector-config/data files. These dependencies are implicit and uncaptured, so a test that 'passes on my machine' can fail elsewhere, and a genuinely-missing optional dependency reports as a hard FAIL instead of a SKIP.

This is deeper than a quick fix; this issue is to remember to design a robust approach.
design:
Initial ideas (not yet decided):

1. SKIP, don't FAIL, on a missing *external/optional* dependency. Use ctest SKIP_RETURN_CODE (test exits 77) or SKIP_REGULAR_EXPRESSION so 'missing DUNE jsonnet' shows as Skipped, keeping the suite green while still visible. Reserve hard failure for genuine regressions.

2. Make each test's environment explicit in CMake via set_tests_properties(<t> PROPERTIES ENVIRONMENT "WIRECELL_PATH=...;PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH=...;LD_LIBRARY_PATH=...") and REQUIRED_FILES "<config>" so ctest reports a clear missing-file rather than a cryptic runtime error. (We already do an ad-hoc version of this in phlex-arrow-hdf's integration_write.sh / integration_drop.sh that set PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH + LD_LIBRARY_PATH — see memory phlex-arrow-write-path-integration. Generalize that pattern.)

3. Classify tests with ctest LABELS: 'unit' (pure, zero external deps — always run) vs 'integration'/'system' (need configured env + plugins + data). CI runs unit always; gates the heavy set behind label + available resources.

4. Vendor small fixtures into the test tree and bundle needed sub-graph jsonnets into each package's cfg/, then point the test's JSONNET/WIRECELL_PATH at them. For configs that legitimately cannot be vendored (DUNE detector geometry, large data), provide a minimal synthetic stand-in used by a self-contained variant of the test, OR gate per #1.

5. A shared test-fixture helper that materializes a temp environment (a scratch dir + JSONNET/WIRECELL_PATH/PHLEX_PLUGIN_PATH composed from build/install dirs and bundled cfg) so individual tests don't hand-roll env setup.

6. Document the environment contract: a per-test (or per-package) manifest of what each test needs (env vars, files, plugins, binaries), so the implicit becomes explicit and auditable.

7. Prefer build/install-relative paths (configured by CMake) over absolute or ambient ones, so tests do not assume a particular container layout (today integration tests assume /devel/ddm/install + the Spack view).
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented policy distinguishing self-contained unit tests from environment-dependent integration/system tests.
- Tests that require an unavailable EXTERNAL resource SKIP (clearly reported) rather than FAIL.
- Required env vars and config files are declared in CMake (ENVIRONMENT / REQUIRED_FILES) or materialized by a fixture, not assumed from the ambient shell.
- The wire-cell-phlex 'phlex_pdhd_sim_sigproc*' tests either run from bundled/synthetic config or are cleanly skipped when the DUNE config is absent.

title: Optional: FORM core remediation (if FORM retained)
description:
OPTIONAL / ALTERNATIVE PATH. If the existing FORM prototype is retained (rather
than superseded by the first-class multi-output-module Arrow design), remediate
its core so it can host technologies like HDF5.

## Context

FORM (`sources/phlex/form/`) currently works only for ROOT TTree and has two
structural shortcomings (see `phlex-file-io-design.md`, ddm umbrella top dir,
section 2):

1. It flattens the structured `data_cell_index` to an integer row before the
   storage backend sees it (the `index` container + `Placement`/`Token` carry
   only file + two-level `creator/label` + int row). HDF5 needs the structured
   address to build a group hierarchy.
2. Its internal technology indirection (`IStorage` matrix) is largely redundant
   with Phlex's native multi-output-module mechanism.

The recommended direction is the first-class output-module Arrow design (the
other issues in this epic), which avoids both problems by construction. This
issue exists ONLY for the scenario where FORM must be kept and fixed in place.

## Deliverables (only if FORM is retained)

1. Widen `Placement`/`Token` and the persistence layer to carry the structured
   `data_cell_index` path (layer names + numbers) to the backend, instead of
   collapsing it to an int row. Keep the row scheme as a within-container
   selector beneath a structured address.
2. Allow arbitrary-depth addressing (replace the two-level `creator/label`
   container name with a structured path) so HDF5 can build `/.../creator/product`.
3. Add a per-write metadata channel so Arrow schema metadata can reach the
   backend.

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` sections 1, 2.
- `sources/phlex/form/core/placement.hpp`, `core/token.hpp`,
  `persistence/persistence_*.cpp`, `storage/istorage.hpp`.

## Acceptance

- Decision recorded on whether FORM is retained. If yes: the backend receives the
  full structured address on both write and read, demonstrated by a test.
- If the first-class Arrow design is adopted instead, close this as won't-do with
  a pointer to that decision.

title: Backlog: arrow-rntuple and arrow-parquet technologies
description:
Backlog: extend the narrow waist to additional file technologies — ROOT RNTuple
and Apache Parquet — by adding pure `arrow-<tech>` libraries and thin
`phlex-arrow-<tech>` packages.

## Context

The scaling rule of the design: adding a technology costs one pure
`arrow-<tech>` (Arrow <-> tech serialization) plus one thin `phlex-arrow-<tech>`
(output module + read side), with NO changes to domain packages or
`phlex-arrow-common`. See `phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir),
section 5. This is a placeholder/backlog for those technologies; split into real
issues when prioritized.

## Candidates

- **arrow-parquet**: should be nearly free — Apache Arrow ships Arrow <->
  Parquet writers/readers. Mostly wiring + the neutral Address -> Parquet
  dataset/file-path mapping.
- **arrow-rntuple**: ROOT RNTuple has its own columnar field schema
  (`RFieldBase`); map the Arrow schema to RNTuple fields. Larger effort; requires
  RNTuple familiarity.

## Notes

- The read sides of these are subject to the same Phlex 0.3.0 deferral as HDF5.
- Each follows the exact pattern of `arrow-hdf` + `phlex-arrow-hdf`; reuse
  `phlex-arrow-common`.

## Acceptance

- This issue is a backlog marker. Acceptance is "split into concrete
  arrow-parquet / arrow-rntuple issues when prioritized."

title: Multi-technology input hierarchy merge/authority [DEFERRED]
description:
DEFERRED until Phlex 0.3.0. Define the policy for reading from MULTIPLE input
file technologies in one job, where several drivers/sources jointly establish a
consistent data-cell hierarchy.

## Context

A job may read some products from HDF5 and others from RNTuple (etc.). Each
technology contributes its own provider(s) and its own file scan / driver. The
drivers must jointly produce ONE consistent cell hierarchy; if two technologies
describe overlapping layers, their generic descriptors must be merged, or one
driver designated authoritative for the hierarchy while others only supply
products. See `phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir), section 8.4.

## Why deferred

Blocked by "Await Phlex 0.3.0". The new model (driver accepts one or more
sources) is precisely the mechanism that will arbitrate multiple inputs, so this
policy must be designed against that API.

## Deliverables (against the 0.3.0 API)

1. A merge/authority policy: how the cell hierarchies discovered by multiple
   technology scans are combined into one the driver yields.
2. Conflict handling (a cell present in one input but not another; layer-name
   mismatches; differing numbering).
3. Configuration to express which input is authoritative for the hierarchy vs
   product-only.

## Dependencies

- "Await Phlex 0.3.0" (blocker).
- Read-side helpers and per-tech read sides (deferred).

## References

- `phlex-file-io-design.md` section 8.4.

## Acceptance

- After 0.3.0: an integration test reads products for the same cells from two
  technologies and reconstructs one consistent hierarchy per the chosen policy.

title: Semantic Arrow schema versioning/governance
description:
Establish schema versioning / governance for the semantic Arrow schemas
(`wc.frame`, `wc.depo`, `wc.tensor`, ...) so files remain readable long term.

## Context

The intermediate Arrow representation embeds semantic schema names and metadata
(e.g. `arrow.schema = wc.frame`, frame scalars in schema metadata). These are the
de-facto on-disk contract regardless of which technology stores them. Without
version discipline, schema evolution silently breaks old-file readability. See
`phlex-file-io-design.md` (ddm umbrella top dir), section 4, and the beads memory
`arrow-frame-companion-table-naming` for the current column/metadata conventions.

## Deliverables

1. A version tag in each semantic schema's metadata (e.g. `wc.frame.version`).
2. A documented compatibility policy (what changes are additive/backward-safe vs
   breaking; how readers detect and handle versions).
3. A registry/document of the current schemas and their versions (extend the
   conventions already captured in beads memory and in
   `wire-cell-arrow/Converters.h`).
4. Reader behavior on unknown/newer versions (reject, warn, best-effort).

## References

- `sources/wire-cell-arrow/wire_cell_arrow/Converters.h`.
- beads memory `arrow-frame-companion-table-naming`.
- `phlex-file-io-design.md` section 4.

## Acceptance

- Every semantic schema carries a version.
- The compatibility policy is documented and a test asserts a versioned schema
  round-trips and that a reader detects a deliberately-bumped version.
notes: DONE + tested (wire-cell-arrow, branch main, commit af32b29). (#1 version tag) every wc.* schema carries arrow.schema.version via central semantic_metadata() helper; kSchemaVersion=1. (#2 policy) docs/schema-versioning.md: additive-vs-breaking rules + reader-behavior table. (#3 registry) doc has the schema table (all v1) + links to fidelity.md/companion-naming memory. title: Factor path-manipulation utilities into their own package (or adopt a library)
description:
arrow-hdf currently carries generic path utilities (path_escape / path_unescape — reversible percent-encoding of '/'-delimited path components — plus path joining in Address and path splitting in Hierarchy). These are not Arrow- or HDF5-specific and are likely reusable by other packages (e.g. phlex-arrow-common, future arrow-<tech> serializers).

## Task
Factor the path-manipulation functions into their own small, dependency-free package under source/ (consumed by arrow-hdf and others), OR adopt a suitable existing C++ library that provides equivalent functionality.

## Notes / candidates
- std::filesystem::path handles join/split/normalization but NOT percent-escaping or the 'safe link-name' charset we need, and carries OS-path semantics we don't want.
- A Python pathlib analog for C++ (lexical, no filesystem) would be ideal: pure lexical '/'-path manipulation + a component-escaping codec.
- Candidates to evaluate: a tiny in-house 'slashpath' package (path_escape/unescape, join, split, parent); or vendoring/adopting an existing lexical-path lib.
- Whatever is chosen must keep the escaping reversible and forbid '/' and NUL in escaped components (current contract). arrow-hdf would then depend on it instead of defining the helpers itself.

Low priority / backlog.
